# follow(_:duration:)

**Type Method**

Creates an action that moves the node along a relative path, orienting the node to the path.

## Declaration

```swift
class func follow(_ path: CGPath, duration: TimeInterval) -> SKAction
```

## Parameters

- **path**: A Core Graphics path whose coordinates are relative to the node’s current position.
- **duration**: The duration of the animation.

## Return Value

A new action object.

## Discussion

Calling this method is equivalent to calling the [doc://com.apple.spritekit/documentation/SpriteKit/SKAction/follow(_:asOffset:orientToPath:duration:)] method, passing in [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] to both the `offset` and `orient` parameters.

This action is reversible; the resulting action creates and then follows a reversed path with the same duration.

## Animating a Node’s Position Along a Custom Path

- **follow(_:speed:)**: Creates an action that moves the node along a relative path at a specified speed, orienting the node to the path.
- **follow(_:asOffset:orientToPath:duration:)**: Creates an action that moves the node along a path.
- **follow(_:asOffset:orientToPath:speed:)**: Creates an action that moves the node at a specified speed along a path.
